title: Listening - Part 3: Trying a New Activity
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about their experience of trying a new outdoor activity. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what each speaker says about the experience. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about their experience of trying a new outdoor activity. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what each speaker says about the experience. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about trying a new outdoor activity.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I tried paddleboarding on holiday. From the beach it looked effortless, but the moment I stood up, I fell straight into the water. I expected the instructor to get impatient, as everybody else seemed far better. [19]Instead, she calmly showed me the same movement several times and waited while I tried again, without ever making me feel foolish.[/19] By the end I could stay upright for a few minutes, and I'd definitely try it again.||Speaker 2 Man: A friend took me climbing at an outdoor wall. I usually avoid activities where there's a chance of looking silly, and at first I apologised every time I couldn't reach the next hold. Nobody cared; they were all busy making their own attempts. [20]After falling safely on the rope a couple of times, I stopped worrying about getting things wrong and became much more willing to try difficult routes.[/20] That change in attitude surprised me.||Speaker 3 Woman: I'd watched people rowing on the lake and assumed it would be a relaxing way to spend a morning. The boat moved smoothly, so it never looked demanding. Then I joined a beginners' session. [21]Within twenty minutes my arms and back were aching, and I couldn't believe how much effort was required just to keep moving in a straight line.[/21] I liked learning the technique, but I certainly slept well that night.||Speaker 4 Man: I signed up for a weekend football session even though I hadn't played since primary school. At first I was worried that the regular players would get annoyed with me for missing easy passes. [22]In fact, the others kept encouraging me, explaining where to stand and celebrating when I finally managed a decent shot.[/22] Their attitude mattered more than the match result. I went home exhausted, but I immediately booked another session.||Speaker 5 Woman: I normally draw at my kitchen table, so I was unsure about joining a sketching walk in the countryside. Carrying materials was slightly inconvenient, and the wind kept turning my pages. [23]But sitting among the trees, hearing birds and watching the sunlight change across the hills made the whole experience far more enjoyable than working indoors.[/23] My finished picture was rather untidy, yet it reminds me of a really peaceful afternoon.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I tried paddleboarding on holiday. From the beach it looked effortless, but the moment I stood up, I fell straight into the water. I expected the instructor to get impatient, as everybody else seemed far better. Instead, she calmly showed me the same movement several times and waited while I tried again, without ever making me feel foolish. By the end I could stay upright for a few minutes, and I'd definitely try it again.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: A friend took me climbing at an outdoor wall. I usually avoid activities where there's a chance of looking silly, and at first I apologised every time I couldn't reach the next hold. Nobody cared; they were all busy making their own attempts. After falling safely on the rope a couple of times, I stopped worrying about getting things wrong and became much more willing to try difficult routes. That change in attitude surprised me.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: I'd watched people rowing on the lake and assumed it would be a relaxing way to spend a morning. The boat moved smoothly, so it never looked demanding. Then I joined a beginners' session. Within twenty minutes my arms and back were aching, and I couldn't believe how much effort was required just to keep moving in a straight line. I liked learning the technique, but I certainly slept well that night.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I signed up for a weekend football session even though I hadn't played since primary school. At first I was worried that the regular players would get annoyed with me for missing easy passes. In fact, the others kept encouraging me, explaining where to stand and celebrating when I finally managed a decent shot. Their attitude mattered more than the match result. I went home exhausted, but I immediately booked another session.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: I normally draw at my kitchen table, so I was unsure about joining a sketching walk in the countryside. Carrying materials was slightly inconvenient, and the wind kept turning my pages. But sitting among the trees, hearing birds and watching the sunlight change across the hills made the whole experience far more enjoyable than working indoors. My finished picture was rather untidy, yet it reminds me of a really peaceful afternoon.

options.text: They were surprised by how tiring it was.
They became less afraid of making mistakes.
They enjoyed having freedom to be creative.
They valued the support of the group.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker praises the instructor for calmly repeating guidance while he learned.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker overcame a fear of failure while trying climbing.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker did not expect rowing to require so much physical effort.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker valued the encouragement received from the other players.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker especially liked drawing outside in the natural surroundings.
